description: Foreign key cascade, restrict, and set-null actions define deletion and update behavior across tables.
body: Use this when adding, removing, or changing foreign keys, delete flows, archival flows, or parent-child relationships. The action attached to a relationship can delete child rows, block parent deletion, or leave nullable references, so it is part of the data contract. This does not decide business retention rules. Execution impact: inspect existing data for orphans, identify application delete paths, choose explicit on-delete/on-update behavior, and test delete/update scenarios. Validation signals include no orphan rows, expected cascade or restrict behavior, application errors mapped cleanly, and migration dry-run success. Risk boundary: cascade delete can cause accidental data loss if applied to high-fanout relationships without count checks. Use when Database Engineering work touches schema deltas, query shape, transaction ownership, constraints, locks, and data volume. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include migration dry-runs, rollback checks, EXPLAIN output, constraint tests, and fixture data comparisons. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves data loss, long locks, non-rollbackable writes, silent query-plan regressions, and unsafe backfills, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
